messages.content: You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
Summarize the purpose of an SFT smoke test in one sentence.
An SFT smoke test checks whether the supervised fine-tuning data path, model loading, forward pass, backward pass, and optimizer step can complete in the current environment.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
Give one operational rule for shared Ascend training servers.
Use only the assigned container and devices, record every command and result, and avoid changing host-level dependencies unless explicitly approved.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
Why should a run report include package versions and command lines?
They make the result reproducible and help distinguish framework issues from configuration or environment changes.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
What is the safest conclusion after a three-step SFT smoke test succeeds?
The training chain can run in this environment, but model quality still needs a longer run and task-specific evaluation.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
Name two signals that a tiny SFT run is healthy.
A healthy tiny SFT run finishes the requested optimizer steps and reports finite loss without device, distributed, or out-of-memory errors.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
Explain why we avoid dependency changes during this test.
Keeping dependencies unchanged isolates whether the current environment already supports the training workflow.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
What should we do if the smoke test fails before the first step?
Record the exact error, command, environment, and device state, then diagnose the smallest failing layer before changing configuration.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
What does LoRA reduce during fine-tuning?
LoRA reduces the number of trainable parameters by learning small adapter matrices while keeping the base model weights frozen.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
What does FSDP help with in large model training?
FSDP shards model states across devices so larger models can train within limited device memory.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
What is a conservative interpretation of a finite loss in a smoke test?
Finite loss means the numerical training path is functioning for the tiny sample, not that the model has learned a useful production behavior.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
Write a short handoff note for a successful SFT smoke run.
The SFT smoke run completed the requested steps with finite loss and no NPU errors; use the logged command and environment as the baseline for longer validation.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
Write a short handoff note for a failed SFT smoke run.
The SFT smoke run did not complete; preserve the log, exit code, device state, and exact command before changing dependencies or retrying.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
Why is max_length important for SFT memory use?
Longer sequences increase activation memory and attention or recurrent state work, so max_length strongly affects whether a run fits in memory.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
What should be recorded after a training smoke test?
Record the container, model path, data path, command, exit code, step count, runtime, loss, memory, and any warnings or errors.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
How do we separate environment validation from quality validation?
Environment validation proves the training process runs, while quality validation requires realistic data, held-out prompts, and task-specific metrics.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
What is the next step after SFT smoke succeeds?
Run a slightly longer controlled training job on representative data and compare adapter outputs against the base model.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
Give a concise definition of supervised fine-tuning.
Supervised fine-tuning trains a model on prompt-response examples so it better imitates the desired answer style and content.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
Why use a tiny dataset for the first SFT check?
A tiny dataset reaches the training path quickly and minimizes resource use while exposing data, model, and distributed setup problems.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
What does exit code zero mean for this smoke test?
Exit code zero means the script completed successfully, but the logged metrics still need review for finite loss and expected step count.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
Why should generated checkpoints be disabled in a smoke test?
Disabling checkpoint saves reduces disk usage and focuses the test on whether training itself can run.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
What is one risk of using synthetic smoke-test data?
Synthetic data can validate plumbing but may produce misleading quality signals if treated like real task evaluation.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
How should we handle a shared server after a smoke test finishes?
Confirm no training processes remain, leave the assigned container in a known state, and document the result for the next operator.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
What does a training wrapper help with?
A wrapper keeps command arguments consistent so later runs can be repeated and compared without reconstructing long commands by hand.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
What is one useful memory metric to record?
Record the peak device memory reported by the training log, because it shows how close the run is to the hardware limit.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
Why not touch other containers during this project?
Other containers may belong to other users or experiments, so touching only our assigned container preserves isolation and avoids disrupting shared work.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
What makes a training result reproducible?
A reproducible result includes stable code, data, model paths, environment versions, command arguments, logs, and a clear run identifier.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
How should we describe a successful smoke test to stakeholders?
Say that the framework can execute the minimal training workflow on the target hardware, while larger-scale stability and quality remain separate checks.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
What should a rollback action include?
A rollback action should identify the last known good command or commit and the exact change to undo before retrying.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
What is one sign that the dataset format is accepted?
The trainer maps the dataset, prints an encoded sample, and starts the training progress bar without schema errors.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
Why keep the first SFT run short?
A short run reduces risk and cost while still proving whether the current software stack can perform supervised training steps.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
What is the difference between smoke success and production readiness?
Smoke success proves the path works at small scale, while production readiness requires robust data, monitoring, evaluation, and repeatable longer runs.
You are a concise training smoke-test assistant.
What is the current project rule about dependencies?
Do not change dependencies during this SFT check; test the environment exactly as it exists and record the result honestly.
